breaking.text: Okta beats Q4 estimates but stock dips after-hours on slower FY27 growth guidance of ~9%
EU expects exemption from imminent U.S. 15% universal tariff hike, sources say
Broadcom guides Q2 revenue to $22B, beating estimates; CEO sees $100B+ AI chip revenue by 2027
Broadcom surges 5% after hours on blowout Q1: AI revenue up 106%, CEO sees $100B AI chip revenue by 2027
S&P 500 futures slide 0.3% Thursday as Brent crude resumes climb toward $84 on Iran escalation fears

summary: Wall Street staged a broad recovery on Wednesday as the S&P 500 rose 0.78%, the Nasdaq jumped 1.29%, and the Dow added 0.49%, driven by easing oil prices and strong ADP jobs data (63K vs 50K expected). After the bell, Broadcom delivered a blowout Q1 with record $19.3B revenue (+29% YoY), AI revenue surging 106% to $8.4B, and announced a $10B buyback alongside Q2 guidance of $22B that crushed estimates — while Okta beat on earnings and revenue but saw shares dip on modest FY27 growth guidance. Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed a 15% global tariff hike is imminent this week, though the EU expects an exemption under its existing trade framework. Overnight futures point to a modest pullback with S&P 500 futures down ~0.25%, as markets digest the earnings wave and tariff uncertainty heading into Thursday's session.

highlights.text: Ross Stores surges 7.4% after Q4 comp sales jump 9% (double estimates), EPS of $2.00 beats guidance; plans 110 new stores in 2026
EU expects to be exempt from U.S. universal tariff hike to 15%, keeping the bloc at 10% under existing trade deal framework
CoreWeave lands multi-year deal to power Perplexity's AI inference on Nvidia GB200 NVL72 clusters, shares pop 8%
Okta beats Q4 with $0.90 EPS vs $0.85 expected and $761M revenue, but stock falls after-hours as FY27 guidance implies growth deceleration to ~9%
Broadcom Q2 revenue guidance of $22B crushes Street estimate of $20.6B; announces $10B share buyback and projects $100B+ AI chip revenue by 2027
